openmeter/billing/adapter.purpose: Ent-backed persistence layer implementing billing.Adapter and its sub-interfaces (CustomerOverrideAdapter, GatheringInvoiceAdapter, SequenceAdapter, etc.) for invoices, lines, profiles, customer overrides, split-line groups, sequence numbers, schema-level tracking, and validation issues. All mutation flows are transaction-aware and namespace-scoped.

patterns.name: Self-rebinding tx adapter
patterns.description: adapter implements Tx/WithTx/Self; every read+write method body wraps in entutils.TransactingRepo or TransactingRepoWithNoValue so it rebinds to the ctx-carried transaction. New methods MUST do the same, never touch a.db directly inside multi-step mutations.
patterns.example: return entutils.TransactingRepo(ctx, a, func(ctx context.Context, tx *adapter) (T, error) { ... tx.db.BillingInvoice.Query()... })

patterns.name: Interface assertion per file
patterns.description: Each file asserts the sub-interface it satisfies via a blank var, e.g. `var _ billing.GatheringInvoiceAdapter = (*adapter)(nil)`. Add the matching assertion when implementing a new adapter sub-interface.
patterns.example: var _ billing.SequenceAdapter = (*adapter)(nil)

patterns.name: Diff-then-upsert for line sets
patterns.description: Line collections are persisted via entitydiff.DiffByID producing create/update/delete buckets, then upsertWithOptions (upsert.go) drives a single CreateBulk + OnConflict. Deletes are modeled as soft-delete updates (MarkDeleted sets DeletedAt) and applied FIRST to satisfy constraints.
patterns.example: upsertWithOptions(ctx, a.db, diff.Line, upsertInput[*billing.GatheringLine, *db.BillingInvoiceLineCreate]{Create: ..., UpsertItems: ..., MarkDeleted: ...})

patterns.name: mapXFromDB / mapXToDB conversions
patterns.description: DB->domain mapping uses free funcs named mapCustomerOverrideFromDB, mapGatheringInvoiceFromDB, etc.; time fields are normalized to UTC via .In(time.UTC) and convert.TimePtrIn; ISO-duration strings parse via ParsePtrOrNil. Follow these names and UTC normalization.
patterns.example: CreatedAt: invoice.CreatedAt.In(time.UTC), DeletedAt: convert.TimePtrIn(invoice.DeletedAt, time.UTC)

patterns.name: Pessimistic locking & sequencing
patterns.description: Per-customer serialization uses BillingCustomerLock upsert (DoNothing) + ForUpdate row lock in LockCustomerForUpdate; sequence numbers use ForUpdate + create-on-missing in NextSequenceNumber. New cross-invoice mutations should take the customer lock first.
patterns.example: tx.db.BillingCustomerLock.Query().Where(...).ForUpdate().First(ctx)

patterns.name: NotFound/Validation domain errors
patterns.description: Map db.IsNotFound to billing.NotFoundError{Entity, Err} and reject illegal mutations with billing.ValidationError; immutable fields (currency, type, customerID) are re-checked against the existing row before update.
patterns.example: return billing.NotFoundError{ID: input.CustomerID, Entity: billing.EntityCustomerOverride, Err: billing.ErrCustomerOverrideNotFound}

patterns.name: Bulk batching to dodge param limits
patterns.description: Bulk inserts chunk by defaultBulkAssignCustomersToProfileBatchSize (derived from 65535 / column count) before CreateBulk+OnConflict, because PostgreSQL caps parameters at 64k.
patterns.example: for _, chunk := range lo.Chunk(creates, defaultBulkAssignCustomersToProfileBatchSize) { ...CreateBulk(chunk...).OnConflict(...).Exec(ctx) }

key_file_guides.role: Config/New constructor and Tx/WithTx/Self via db.HijackTx + entutils.NewTxDriver
key_file_guides.watch_for: Config.Validate requires non-nil Client and Logger; WithTx must rebuild via NewTxClientFromRawConfig, never reuse a.db
Delete bucket processed first as soft-delete; only runs UpsertItems when buckets non-empty

key_file_guides.role: Gathering invoice line diff/upsert + DB<->domain mapping
key_file_guides.watch_for: Only InvoiceLineAdapterTypeUsageBased + ParentLineIDIsNil lines are gathering lines; ULID assigned when ID/UBPConfigID empty

key_file_guides.role: Split-line group create/get and SplitLineHierarchy resolution
key_file_guides.watch_for: Hierarchy expansion is a separate query path; keep namespace filters on every edge load
Create-on-missing uses OnConflict().DoNothing() then re-reads; returns alpacadecimal.Decimal
Still triggers in-band invoice schema migration (shouldInvoicesBeMigrated/migrateCustomerInvoices) — temporary, do not remove blindly
Hash covers severity+code+message+component+path; IntrospectValidationIssues is test-only, not on the interface

key_file_guides.role: Standard-invoice line <-> DB mapping (detailed + UBP lines)
key_file_guides.watch_for: Companion to stdinvoicelinediff.go; keep mapper and diff in sync when adding line fields

anti_patterns: Calling a.db.* directly for multi-step mutations instead of wrapping in entutils.TransactingRepo(WithNoValue) and using tx.db
Returning raw db errors for not-found/invalid states instead of billing.NotFoundError / billing.ValidationError
Persisting line collections with ad-hoc loops instead of entitydiff.DiffByID + upsertWithOptions
Storing/returning non-UTC times — every timestamp must be .In(time.UTC) on map-in and map-out
Single CreateBulk of unbounded customer/line slices without lo.Chunk batching (hits the 64k param ceiling)

decisions.decision: Transaction hijacking (HijackTx/WithTx) instead of passing tx clients
decisions.rationale: Lets service-layer orchestration share one Ent transaction across many adapter calls via context, keeping invoice mutations atomic

decisions.decision: Validation issues deduplicated by content hash with soft-delete
decisions.rationale: Re-running calculation re-asserts the same issues; hashing avoids duplicate rows while pruning issues no longer present

decisions.decision: Gathering invoices reuse the BillingInvoice table with UNSET supplier/customer hacks
decisions.rationale: Tables not yet split; UpdateGatheringInvoice clears supplier/customer columns as an interim until a dedicated gathering table exists

code_examples.scenario: Transaction-aware adapter method returning a domain value

openmeter/billing/httpdriver.purpose: Legacy v1 HTTP transport for billing: translates api.* request/response types to/from billing domain inputs and delegates to billing.Service. Pure adapter layer — no business logic, only mapping, namespace resolution, and error encoding.

patterns.name: httptransport handler triple
patterns.description: Each endpoint is a method on *handler returning a typed httptransport.Handler[WithArgs] built from (request decoder, business call, response encoder) + AppendOptions(h.options, WithOperationName, WithErrorEncoder). Request/Response/Params are declared as type aliases at the top of each file.
patterns.example: return httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs(decode, func(ctx, in) (Resp,error){ return h.service.ListCustomerOverrides(ctx, in) }, commonhttp.JSONResponseEncoderWithStatus[Resp](http.StatusOK), httptransport.AppendOptions(h.options, ...))

patterns.name: Interface-segmented Handler
patterns.description: The public Handler interface composes ProfileHandler, InvoiceLineHandler, InvoiceHandler, CustomerOverrideHandler; each method returns a typed handler constructor. New endpoints must be added to the matching sub-interface and implemented on *handler.
patterns.example: type Handler interface { ProfileHandler; InvoiceLineHandler; InvoiceHandler; CustomerOverrideHandler }

patterns.name: Namespace from decoder
patterns.description: Every decoder calls h.resolveNamespace(ctx) which reads namespacedriver.NamespaceDecoder; failure returns a 500 HTTPError. Inputs are always namespace-scoped.
patterns.example: ns, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx); if err != nil { return Req{}, fmt.Errorf(...) }

patterns.name: Centralized billing error encoder
patterns.description: errorEncoder() chains commonhttp.HandleErrorIfTypeMatches for billing.NotFoundError(404), ValidationError(400), UpdateAfterDeleteError(409), ValidationIssue(400), all rendered via billing.EncodeValidationIssues. Domain errors must be one of these types to map to the right status.
patterns.example: commonhttp.HandleErrorIfTypeMatches[billing.ValidationError](ctx, http.StatusBadRequest, err, w, billing.EncodeValidationIssues)

patterns.name: API<->entity mapping via lo pointer helpers
patterns.description: Decoders unwrap optional api fields with lo.FromPtr / lo.FromPtrOr and build billing input structs; per-item conversions use slicesx.MapWithErr and dedicated mapXToAPI / mapXToEntity funcs.
patterns.example: OrderBy: billing.CustomerOverrideOrderBy(lo.FromPtrOr(input.OrderBy, ...)), CustomerIDs: lo.FromPtr(input.CustomerId)

patterns.name: ValidationIssues surfaced as errors
patterns.description: When a returned invoice carries ValidationIssues, the handler converts them to a ValidationError via invoice.ValidationIssues.AsError() rather than returning a 200 with issues.
patterns.example: if len(invoice.ValidationIssues) > 0 { return Resp{}, billing.ValidationError{Err: invoice.ValidationIssues.AsError()} }

key_file_guides.role: Handler interface composition, handler struct, New constructor, resolveNamespace
key_file_guides.watch_for: handler holds service, appService, namespaceDecoder, featureSwitches (config.BillingFeatureSwitchesConfiguration), options; New also takes stripeAppService

key_file_guides.role: errorEncoder() mapping billing domain errors to HTTP statuses
key_file_guides.watch_for: Order matters — first matching type wins; all use billing.EncodeValidationIssues
ProgressInvoice is parameterized by ProgressAction; surfaces invoice ValidationIssues as ValidationError
Empty req.Lines returns billing.ValidationError; lines mapped per-item with slicesx.MapWithErr
MapProfileToApi resolves app references via appService; keep nil-profile guards
mapCustomerOverrideExpandToEntity translates api expand enums; reuses customerhttpdriver for nested customer mapping

key_file_guides.role: Backward-compat shims for deprecated request shapes
key_file_guides.watch_for: Has deprecations_test.go — keep shim behavior covered when changing field handling

anti_patterns: Putting business logic (state transitions, calculation) in handlers instead of delegating to billing.Service
Returning bare errors instead of billing.NotFoundError/ValidationError/ValidationIssue (breaks HTTP status mapping)
Reading the namespace manually instead of h.resolveNamespace(ctx)
Returning a 200 response while invoice.ValidationIssues is non-empty
Hand-writing pointer/optional unwrapping instead of lo.FromPtr/FromPtrOr and slicesx.MapWithErr

decisions.decision: All handlers built through httptransport with a shared errorEncoder
decisions.rationale: Uniform error-to-status mapping and operation naming/telemetry across every billing endpoint without per-handler boilerplate

decisions.decision: Request/Response/Params declared as type aliases of api.* and billing.* inputs
decisions.rationale: Keeps the transport layer a thin, statically-checked mapping between OpenAPI types and domain inputs

code_examples.scenario: A billing HTTP handler delegating to the service

openmeter/billing/lineengine.purpose: Concrete line Engine implementing billing.LineEngine + billing.LineCalculator: builds standard invoice lines from gathering lines, splits gathering lines at period boundaries, snapshots quantities, resolves split-line-group hierarchy, and generates/merges detailed rated lines. This is the lifecycle-hook engine the service registers for LineEngineTypeInvoice.

patterns.name: Config+Validate+New constructor
patterns.description: Engine is built via New(Config) where Config.Validate() requires non-nil SplitLineGroupAdapter, QuantitySnapshotter, and rating.Service; constructor returns error, never panics.
patterns.example: func New(config Config) (*Engine, error) { if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }; return &Engine{...}, nil }

patterns.name: Engine satisfies both billing interfaces
patterns.description: var _ billing.LineEngine and var _ billing.LineCalculator both assert *Engine. Lifecycle hooks (OnCollectionCompleted, OnStandardInvoiceCreated, OnInvoiceIssued, OnPayment*) must keep matching signatures; unused hooks return input.Lines / nil.
patterns.example: var ( _ billing.LineEngine = (*Engine)(nil); _ billing.LineCalculator = (*Engine)(nil) )

patterns.name: Snapshot gating before quantity capture
patterns.description: OnCollectionCompleted skips snapshotting when QuantitySnapshotedAt is already past the default collection time, or when collection time is still in the future; only then calls quantitySnapshotter.SnapshotLineQuantities.
patterns.example: if input.Invoice.QuantitySnapshotedAt != nil && !input.Invoice.QuantitySnapshotedAt.Before(input.Invoice.DefaultCollectionAtForStandardInvoice()) { return input.Lines, nil }

patterns.name: Split-at into pre/post lines via CloneForCreate
patterns.description: SplitGatheringLine creates a SplitLineGroup if absent, then derives postSplitAtLine via line.CloneForCreate, trims the original to [from, splitAt], and soft-deletes either side whose truncated period is empty (FlatPrice never empty; usage truncated to streaming.MinimumWindowSizeDuration).
patterns.example: postSplitAtLine, err := line.CloneForCreate(func(l *billing.GatheringLine){ l.ServicePeriod.From = in.SplitAt; l.SplitLineGroupID = lo.ToPtr(splitLineGroupID); l.ChildUniqueReferenceID = nil })

patterns.name: Detailed-line generation then merge+validate
patterns.description: CalculateLines loops standard lines, calls ratingService.GenerateDetailedLines, then invoicecalc.MergeGeneratedDetailedLines, then stdLine.Validate(); any per-line error is wrapped with the line ID.
patterns.example: generated, _ := e.ratingService.GenerateDetailedLines(stdLine); invoicecalc.MergeGeneratedDetailedLines(stdLine, generated)

key_file_guides.watch_for: Snapshot failures of type *billing.ErrSnapshotInvalidDatabaseState are converted to a critical billing.ValidationIssue, not a raw error
splitAt must lie within line.ServicePeriod; empty-after-truncation sides are soft-deleted; isPeriodEmptyConsideringTruncations special-cases FlatPriceType
Build path requires non-empty Invoice.ID and GatheringLines; ToStandardLines then ResolveSplitLineGroupHeaders then snapshot, in that order

anti_patterns: panic on missing engine type or inputs in production paths — return errors (only the testutils Noop panics)
Skipping invoicecalc.MergeGeneratedDetailedLines / stdLine.Validate() after generating detailed lines
Splitting a line at a point outside its ServicePeriod, or reusing ChildUniqueReferenceID on the post-split clone
Returning a raw error for snapshot DB-state failures instead of a billing.ValidationIssue
Adding a LineEngine hook to the interface without updating *Engine (and testutils.NoopLineEngine)

decisions.decision: Engine carries a SplitLineGroupAdapter and QuantitySnapshotter as injected collaborators
decisions.rationale: Keeps persistence (split groups) and metering (quantity snapshot) behind narrow interfaces so the engine stays a pure orchestration unit

decisions.decision: Snapshot DB-state errors become critical ValidationIssues
decisions.rationale: Lets the invoice surface a recoverable metering problem to the user instead of failing the whole pipeline opaquely

code_examples.scenario: Building standard lines: snapshot then rate then validate

openmeter/billing/testutils.purpose: Shared test doubles for the billing domain. Currently provides NoopLineEngine, a no-op billing.LineEngine for embedding in line-engine fakes; it deliberately does NOT implement billing.LineCalculator.

patterns.name: Noop engine implementing every hook
patterns.description: NoopLineEngine satisfies var _ billing.LineEngine and returns pass-through results (input.Lines, nil) for every lifecycle hook; Build* methods just call input.GatheringLines.ToStandardLines(input.Invoice.ID).
patterns.example: func (NoopLineEngine) OnCollectionCompleted(_ context.Context, input billing.OnCollectionCompletedInput) (billing.StandardLines, error) { return input.Lines, nil }

patterns.name: Required EngineType via panic
patterns.description: GetLineEngineType panics when EngineType is the empty string — tests must set NoopLineEngine{EngineType: ...}. Panic is acceptable here because it is test-only code.
patterns.example: func (e NoopLineEngine) GetLineEngineType() billing.LineEngineType { if e.EngineType == "" { panic("engine type is required") }; return e.EngineType }

key_file_guides.role: NoopLineEngine struct + all billing.LineEngine hook implementations
key_file_guides.watch_for: Intentionally omits LineCalculator (BuildStandardInvoiceLines exists but CalculateLines does not); embed it and add the calculator separately when a fake needs calculation

anti_patterns: Importing application wiring (app/common) into test helpers here — keep testutils dependent only on openmeter/billing
Using NoopLineEngine without setting EngineType (panics on GetLineEngineType)

decisions.decision: Noop engine is a struct with an exported EngineType field rather than a constructor
decisions.rationale: Lets tests pick the engine type inline and embed the noop in larger fakes that override only specific hooks
